items.title: Iran’s security chief, Ali Larijani, killed in airstrike, Israel says
items.standfirst: If confirmed, death would make Larijani the most senior Iranian figure to be killed since Ali Khamenei on first day of warMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael says it has killed a linchpin of Iranian politics, the national security chief, Ali Larijani, in overnight strikes, a claim that if confirmed would make him the most senior Iranian figure to die in the war since the supreme leader Ali Khamenei was killed on its first day.Iran has yet to comment on either claim. If confirmed, Larijani’s death would remove a pivotal figure at the heart of the regime’s political and security establishment at a moment of acute crisis and represent devastating blow. Continue reading...

If confirmed, Larijani’s death would remove a pivotal figure at the heart of the regime’s political and security establishment at a moment of acute crisis and represent devastating blow. items.title: Israel says killed Iran national security chief Larijani
items.standfirst: Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Tuesday the Israeli military had killed Iran's security chief and the ​head of ‌its Basij militia in airstrikes overnight, and Tehran kept up attacks ⁠against Gulf neighbours that have pushed up energy prices. Katz said in a statement he had been informed by the military that ‌Iran's security chief Ali Larijani had been killed.
items.content: Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Tuesday the Israeli military had killed Iran's security chief and the ​head of ‌its Basij militia in airstrikes overnight, and Tehran kept up attacks ⁠against Gulf neighbours that have pushed up energy prices. Katz said in a statement he had been informed by the military that ‌Iran's security chief Ali Larijani had been killed.

items.title: Israel says it killed two top Iranian commanders in targeted strike
items.standfirst: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani, the highest profile assassinations since the targeting of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on the first day of the war.
items.content: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani, the highest profile assassinations since the targeting of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on the first day of the war.(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)

items.title: Middle East war live: Israel killed Iran’s security chief Ali Larijani, Defence Minister Katz says
items.standfirst: Israel has killed Iran's powerful national security chief Ali Larijani in an overnight strike, Defence Minister Israel Katz said Tuesday. Gholamreza Soleimani, the head of Iran's volunteer Basij paramilitary force, was reportedly also killed in a strike. Iran has not yet commented on Israel's claims. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Israel has killed Iran's powerful national security chief Ali Larijani in an overnight strike, Defence Minister Israel Katz said Tuesday. Gholamreza Soleimani, the head of Iran's volunteer Basij paramilitary force, was reportedly also killed in a strike. Iran has not yet commented on Israel's claims. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
items.standfirst: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.
items.content: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

clusters.title: 'Like doomsday': Pakistan air strike kills at least 400 in Kabul drug rehab centre, Taliban says
clusters.neutral_headline: Afghanistan says 400 killed in strike by Pakistan on Kabul hospital
bullet_summary: A Pakistani air strike on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul killed at least 400 people and wounded more than 250 on Monday night, the Taliban government said Tuesday
Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesperson Hamdullah Fitrat said the death toll had “so far” reached 400 people, while about 250 people had been reported injured
He said most of those killed and wounded were patients undergoing treatment at the facility
Afghanistan has claimed 400 people have been killed in a Pakistan strike which Kabul said hit a hospital that treats drug addicts

items.title: 'Like doomsday': Pakistan air strike kills at least 400 in Kabul drug rehab centre, Taliban says
items.standfirst: A Pakistani air strike on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul killed at least 400 people and wounded more than 250 on Monday night, the Taliban government said Tuesday. Islamabad last month launched a military operation against Afghanistan last month, accusing the government of sheltering extremist groups.
items.content: A Pakistani air strike on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul killed at least 400 people and wounded more than 250 on Monday night, the Taliban government said Tuesday. Islamabad last month launched a military operation against Afghanistan last month, accusing the government of sheltering extremist groups.

items.title: Afghanistan says 400 killed in strike by Pakistan on Kabul hospital
items.standfirst: Deputy government spokesman says death toll has reached 400 people ‘so far’ as Islamabad denies targeting facility for drug addictsHundreds were feared dead after a strike on a hospital treating drug users in the Afghan capital of Kabul, which officials from Afghanistan blamed on the Pakistani military.Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesperson Hamdullah Fitrat said the death toll had “so far” reached 400 people, while about 250 people had been reported injured. He said most of those killed and wounded were patients undergoing treatment at the facility. Continue reading...
items.content: Deputy government spokesman says death toll has reached 400 people ‘so far’ as Islamabad denies targeting facility for drug addictsHundreds were feared dead after a strike on a hospital treating drug users in the Afghan capital of Kabul, which officials from Afghanistan blamed on the Pakistani military.Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesperson Hamdullah Fitrat said the death toll had “so far” reached 400 people, while about 250 people had been reported injured. He said most of those killed and wounded were patients undergoing treatment at the facility. Continue reading...

items.title: French parties scramble to make alliances ahead of local election runoffs
items.standfirst: Local elections, including the race for the mayor of Paris, are set to be decided in runoff votes across France on Sunday with parties still in the running scrambling to make alliances with defeated rivals. They include controversial alliances between the he hard-left France Unbowed and France's Socialist party. Clovis Casali has more details.
items.content: Local elections, including the race for the mayor of Paris, are set to be decided in runoff votes across France on Sunday with parties still in the running scrambling to make alliances with defeated rivals. They include controversial alliances between the he hard-left France Unbowed and France's Socialist party. Clovis Casali has more details.

items.title: French political parties seek alliances before final round of local elections
items.standfirst: Candidates look for deals with rivals to boost chances as major seats including Paris, Marseille and Lyon appear tightPolitical parties in France are hastily attempting to negotiate strategic alliances before the final round of local elections this weekend, after a strong showing by the far right and the radical left.This Sunday’s final-round vote for mayors and local councillors in major cities including Marseille, Lyon and Paris is expected to be close. clusters.title: Trump asks China if visit to Beijing can be delayed a month due to Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump asks China if visit to Beijing can be delayed a month due to Iran war
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, NBC World

items.title: Trump asks China if visit to Beijing can be delayed a month due to Iran war
items.standfirst: US president had earlier hinted trip could be put on hold if President Xi does not help unblock the strait of HormuzMiddle East crisis – live updatesTrump has asked to delay his planned visit to Beijing by about a month due to the Iran war, after earlier hinting he might put the trip off if his prospective hosts do not help to unblock the strait of Hormuz.The US president’s summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meant to take place at the end of March but Trump told reporters in the White House on Monday: “Because of the war I want to be here, I have to be here, I feel. And so we’ve requested that we delay it a month or so.” Continue reading...
items.content: US president had earlier hinted trip could be put on hold if President Xi does not help unblock the strait of HormuzMiddle East crisis – live updatesTrump has asked to delay his planned visit to Beijing by about a month due to the Iran war, after earlier hinting he might put the trip off if his prospective hosts do not help to unblock the strait of Hormuz.The US president’s summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meant to take place at the end of March but Trump told reporters in the White House on Monday: “Because of the war I want to be here, I have to be here, I feel. And so we’ve requested that we delay it a month or so.” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says U.S. wants to delay his meeting with Xi in China by 'a month or so' due to Iran war
items.standfirst: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent cast doubt on the planned trip earlier, while insisting it was not meant to pressure China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent cast doubt on the planned trip earlier, while insisting it was not meant to pressure China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
